title: Eva or Bebop If You had To Choose
display_title: Eva or Bebop If You had To Choose
wikitext:
[]: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Sat Dec 21, 2024 4:58 pm Assuming you've watched both series, and were given a choice of re-watching only one, what do you pick. That's not to say you're limited to the two, but rather between the two. Though by choosing one, you'd lose the other, lowering your options, but that's unimportant. I've said this before: within Japanese animation, Eva's presentation is unmatched, though with regard to episode titles, Bebop is no less memorable. E.g. One More Final: I Need You, Both of You: Dance Like You Want to Win, A Human Work, ect. None read like ANIME (Bebop included), but instead expressions, which is a positive, considering the negative stereotypes associated with Japanese animation (and rightfully so). E.g. Body Pillow, anime girl figurines. I mean, I have an F-18 & F-86 die-cast model. Another stark contrast between the two (titles notwithstanding) is the fact that one's completely organic (Eva), while the other makes use of low-resolution graphics, which stick out like a sore thumb, unlike Macross Plus, whose can be enjoyed a couple decades later. Music (Eva) goes without saying. Admittedly I'm biased, as I'm not a fan of the Jazz bits, or rather they've no appeal; love the individual instruments that make up a track, e.g. Saxophone. Said instrument in-particular adds wonderful feeling to music when used in song Never Tear Us Apart for example. But Bad Dog, No Biscuits, Rush... ehh, suppose if I were in an elevator. Eva at its best matches anything in bebop, e.g. THE splitting of the breast, or for that matter other animations, both Japan & West. However, I think it's accurate in describing Bebop as having fewer low-points than that of Eva. Vehicles (Bebop) when compared to Evangelions, always look clean and polished, whereas an Eva's illustration can appear crude, and in-precise, and whose upper-body proportion (chest) can look off when compared to its lower body & arms. Bebop also scales nicely with pilot out-of cockpit, standing in-view of their ship. In retrospect, I appreciate the original's ending (Eva), and hold its Renewal (Kiba 1008) with the same reverence as I do movie (Kiba 1009/10). Still, whereas Eva treats its finale like it were an earlier episode, Bebop gives you the full affect. Bummer too, because what could have been. That is to say: Cruel Angel Thesis and the non-lyrical renditions heard playing in-background whilst Shinji speaks- that should have continued onward like bebop's Blue. Having the Japanese Renewal editions (DVD) of both Eva & Bebop, Kiba 1001-1010 & BCBA-3195 respectively, it's a sophie's choice kind of thing lol. Bebop is great, but it doesn't have the organic cityscapes of Tokyo-3 or Nerve's Geofront, or Misato's red jacket, the former of which whose precision & detail match Akia's Neo-Tokyo. A fun discussion within fandom.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat Dec 21, 2024 7:27 pm Unless my Asuka figurines comes alive one day and attack me like it's Me! Me! Me! I don't see what's wrong with them. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by ErgoProxy » Sat Dec 21, 2024 8:15 pm Bebop's final fight from Knocking to the Heavens Door is absolute trump. But so is the sound of Lance of Longinus coming back from the Moon, through all those winds and clouds, and whatnot. Read: both series have their perks, and they complement each other surprisingly well. A collab of the studios would be an absolute skating Gojira. That said, Bebop is too melancholic for me. I like melancholy, but this piece of art is... oversaturated? It reeks of sadness; it does the thing very well and almost too well. It's on the verge of being unwatchable at times, because it utterly destroys so many characters with no hope for them and knowing that in their last moments. On the other hand, Eva feels like home. There's a difference between loneliness and solitude, and frankly, I didn't feel, not even once, the worse of those two during the whole series. Like, some people are born to embrace being lonely. Why is that even a thing for an average Joe/Takeshi? To quote Kaworu, I do not understand.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Sat Dec 21, 2024 10:50 pm ErgoProxy wrote: Bebop's final fight from Knocking to the Heavens Door is absolute trump. But so is the sound of Lance of Longinus coming back from the Moon, through all those winds and clouds, and whatnot.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Sat Dec 21, 2024 11:05 pm kuribo-04 wrote: Unless my Asuka figurines comes alive one day and attack me like it's Me! Me! Me! I don't see what's wrong with them. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by ErgoProxy » Sun Dec 22, 2024 2:07 am brandneweyes wrote: At the risk of looking like an idiot, can you elaborate? I'm aware of the movie, Cowboy Bebop: The Movie. I admittedly have a negative view of the film. Lol. Wikipedia gives me a title of Cowboy Bebop: Knockin' on Heaven's Door.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Sun Dec 22, 2024 1:28 pm I'd argue it took away from the original. I asked myself two questions, which were 1: Didn't Spike die? And 2. Didn't Ed leave? I can't name a source, because it's barely a memory, the explanation being the events portrayed in the film happened off-screen in the series, despite there being no mention, and of course no showing of them. He essentially turned his own work into a piece of fan fiction. The Swordfish animation is spectacular, and people look good, but due to the series's perfect finale, good looks aside, in the words of Bueller: "It's Over. Go home." Yeah. The setting of Eva lends itself nicely to such sounds. I think a reason for Eva's FEEL, is the fact that setting takes place in the 2000s, inside a modern city. Now of course the detail & precision is nothing short of grand, and speaks to your point about the two series complimenting one another. Whereas Bebop gives you on-going action, Eva's Renewal will draw your attention to its hyper-realized city, whose precision & detail match Akira's Tokyo-3. I can't take Japanese animation seriously (animation that's spoken in Japanese at least), the way a child would a movie that causes tears, due to caricatures, but I can understand what you mean. And speaking of Jupiter Jazz: Yeah. Bebop has Jupiter Jazz, which is as valuable as EoE. The kid aspect is just wishful thinking watching as an adult.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Sun Dec 22, 2024 7:33 pm As a big fan of both series, I would still go with Evangelion. The reason being that its characters are more fully realized and the story delves deeper into its own themes. I can pinpoint everything I love and hate about each of them. They look very real and I think this adds to the story and makes it more memorable. Both series have great OSTs too, although I like EVA's a bit more. The animation of Bebop is better and its visual style is very memorable. The movie is great too, the ending is very beautiful. But I still prefer EOE. Actually, EOE may as well just be what breaks the tie.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Sun Dec 22, 2024 8:38 pm BernardoCairo wrote: As a big fan of both series, I would still go with Evangelion. The reason being that its characters are more fully realized and the story delves deeper into its own themes. I can pinpoint everything I love and hate about each of them. They look very real and I think this adds to the story and makes it more memorable. Both series have great OSTs too, although I like EVA's a bit more. The animation of Bebop is better and its visual style is very memorable. The movie is great too, the ending is very beautiful. But I still prefer EOE. Actually, EOE may as well just be what breaks the tie.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Dec 26, 2024 1:00 pm I agree. I'm no way saying that Bebop's cast is weak. They're all great. Spike and Faye are particularly fun and iconic. Their backstories are just way too great. But all the others are awesome as well. I love the fridge episode because of their dynamics. It's funny as all hell!

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Mon Dec 30, 2024 12:36 pm I'm not sure I get the comparison--is it just that they're both famous? They both have completely different artistic goals; Bebop is far more anchored in traditional story and character approaches. I think a better comparison from the same scriptwriter as Bebop would actually be Wolf's Rain, because it's also a mid- and post-apocalyptic story and it gets heavily bleak and surreal as it goes, particularly at the end. You could even say it shares the honor of having a troubled (but maybe, ultimately, why it's so good) production history. The studio was affected by an epidemic outbreak. There's a moment in the series where the quality, and the sense of purpose, shoot up in an instant owing to the wrecking ball effect this had and the hiatus it mandated. Notoriously, there are four recap episodes in a row at this time, but then the first episode after staff resume in earnest is almost like out of a different series, and everything strengthens dramatically. I'd pick Eva over all three, but for anyone who hasn't seen Wolf's Rain, I think it's a no-brainer that it would be up an Eva fan's alley. It's one of very few anime to have touched me on a deep level.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Tue Dec 31, 2024 8:20 am Axx°N N. wrote: I'm not sure I get the comparison--is it just that they're both famous?

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Dec 31, 2024 11:22 am I have something of a strange relationship with Bebop, I couldn't get into it and dropped the series midway, even though I could recognize its quality. As I recall I was in something of a bad mood and too busy at the time. Will give it a watch again... someday... Meanwhile Eva resonated (way too much) with me when I watched it in high school, it was a bad year for me for several reasons but somehow Eva clicked with that moment in my life.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Tue Dec 31, 2024 12:24 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: I have something of a strange relationship with Bebop, I couldn't get into it and dropped the series midway, even though I could recognize its quality. As I recall I was in something of a bad mood and too busy at the time. Will give it a watch again... someday... Meanwhile Eva resonated (way too much) with me when I watched it in high school, it was a bad year for me for several reasons but somehow Eva clicked with that moment in my life.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jan 01, 2025 4:18 am Back around the turn of the century, the local art-house cinema had a short season of anime films, which was my introduction to Ghibli, and, as it happens, Bebop. I found that movie unremarkable; my main memory being of the uncomfortable tittering in the audience when the track Touch the Sky started playing over some action scene. Scanning the manga, it didn't inspire, and the OP track Tank, so often lauded, sounded just like any 70s/80s actioner TV series theme music. So it rested. A little while down the line, Eva came with the recommendation that if you only watch one giant robot series, make it that one. So I did, and it just clicked - Asuka, especially, hitting too close to teenage me. The results, you can see around you. A while back, I did start Bebop, but the first episode came over so much as the wannabe American TV show that the OP track was promising that I just wasn't interested.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Wed Jan 01, 2025 3:58 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Back around the turn of the century, the local art-house cinema had a short season of anime films, which was my introduction to Ghibli, and, as it happens, Bebop. I found that movie unremarkable;

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Jan 01, 2025 4:24 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I found that movie unremarkable... Scanning the manga, it didn't inspire, and the OP track Tank, so often lauded, sounded just like any 70s/80s actioner TV series theme music. So it rested. ... A while back, I did start Bebop, but the first episode came over so much as the wannabe American TV show that the OP track was promising that I just wasn't interested.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Wed Jan 01, 2025 5:23 pm "Overall, Bebop has some structural flaws for me. It's a generally good show, but it's worthwhile for a dozen or so stellar episodes, and its back half in particular is enough to make up for it all." I wish Julia's incorporation was better. Instead of a silly death, would've been cool to see her at the end fighting with Spike towards his final confrontation with Vicious. Also, and this is me completely speaking out loud, EoE ends in the same fashion as the series, albeit a different mood, in that neither feature a specialized outro. Lol. It's actually rather humorous; like someone correcting a mistake, but overlooking others. As I said though, in retrospect, I've changed (t.v.). Bebop t.v. however gives you TWO fantastic finales, the other, FINALE, being Space Lion.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Jan 01, 2025 8:51 pm brandneweyes wrote: I wish Julia's incorporation was better. Instead of a completely silly death, would've been cool to see her at the end fighting with Spike towards his final confrontation with Vicious.

[]: Re: Eva or Bebop: If You had To Choose             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Wed Jan 01, 2025 8:58 pm On the other hand, Eva feels like home. Apologies for not acknowledging that point, but yeah, that; it's a combination of other factors that brings it about.
